name: Protect Debug, Actuator, Metrics, and Internal Endpoints
objective: Restrict access to common debug, actuator, metrics, and internal operational endpoints that should not be broadly reachable from the internet.
risk_mitigated: Exposure of operational metadata, health endpoints, diagnostics, and internal surfaces that support reconnaissance or post-auth exploitation.
potential_side_effects: Legitimate platform probes, uptime checks, and internal observability agents can be blocked if their source IPs or service paths are not excluded.
tuning_notes: Add explicit allowlists for uptime monitoring, synthetic probes, and internal platform ranges. If actuator paths are required by a trusted service, scope the allowlist narrowly by source and hostname.
deployment_notes: Deploy directly in block mode only after validating trusted probe and operations ranges. For mixed-use environments, begin in log mode and review all matches for one week.
monitoring_notes: Alert on any request volume against /actuator, /debug, /internal, /metrics, /healthz, and related paths from unknown ASNs or unexpected geographies. Correlate repeated hits with bot score and user-agent anomalies.
